decisions.briefing: Markets are sharply lower today with NASDAQ falling 3.8% and the S&P 500 down 2.4% — a classic risk-off session where investors are fleeing equities. The screener returned zero candidates, which reinforces staying defensive. No new buys today; existing positions remain intact and the auto stop-loss system will handle any that breach the -7% threshold.

market_education.summary_en: Today's broad selloff reflects a risk-off rotation where investors moved away from equities — particularly in growth and tech — toward safer assets like bonds and cash. The NASDAQ's outsized drop of 3.8% versus the S&P 500's 2.4% decline indicates high-multiple tech and growth stocks bore the brunt of selling pressure. When risk appetite collapses this quickly, even fundamentally sound companies see their stock prices fall as institutional investors de-risk portfolios regardless of individual company quality.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場出現明顯的避險情緒，投資者大幅撤出股市，尤其是成長股與科技板塊，轉向債券與現金等避風港資產。納斯達克指數重挫3.8%，遠超標普500的2.4%跌幅，反映出高估值科技股承受了最大的拋售壓力。當市場風險偏好急速收縮時，即使基本面健康的企業股價亦難逃下跌，因為機構投資者不分股票優劣，一律進行去風險操作。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' session is when investors collectively decide to reduce exposure to risky assets like stocks — especially volatile ones like tech — and move into safer havens like government bonds, gold, or cash. You can spot it when the NASDAQ falls much harder than the broader S&P 500, since the NASDAQ holds more growth stocks that investors dump first when fear spikes. Today's -3.8% NASDAQ versus -2.4% S&P 500 is a textbook example.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資者集體決定減少對高風險資產（如股票，尤其是科技成長股）的持倉，轉而買入政府債券、黃金或持有現金等避風港資產的市場狀態。辨識方法之一，是觀察納斯達克指數的跌幅是否遠大於標普500——因為納斯達克集中了更多高增長股票，當恐慌情緒升溫時，這些股票往往是最先被拋售的。今日納斯達克跌3.8%、標普500跌2.4%，正是教科書式的避險情緒案例。
